commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Harris Teeter pharmacies throughout the Triangle. Costco pharmacy in multiple locations. Mail-order options through Medicare Part D plans.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or Medicare Advantage with drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions with tiered copays. Generic drugs $0-15/month, preferred brands $30-50/month, specialty drugs 25-33% coinsurance up to $2,000 annual cap.

sources.title: Inflation Reduction Act Drug Provisions - KFF

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month and cover annual exam + $100-200 frame/lens allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine eye exams. Excellent availability of optometrists. Duke Eye Center and UNC Kittner Eye Center for specialist care (2-4 week waits).
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. One pair of eyeglasses or contacts after cataract surgery.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental of North Carolina, Cigna) typically cover 100% preventive, 50-80% basic, 50% major procedures. Annual maximums $1,000-2,000.
dental.dentistAvailability: Good in Triangle metro. UNC School of Dentistry offers reduced-cost dental care. Wait times typically 1-3 weeks for routine appointments.

sources.title: UNC School of Dentistry - Patient Care

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP restaurant discounts available at many chains.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$32 + 7.25% tax + 20% tip = ~$41/meal, 2x/week = ~$330/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Google Fiber and AT&T Fiber available in Raleigh-Durham.
categories.notes: Google Fiber 1 Gig $70/mo or AT&T Fiber $55-80/mo. Google Fiber is standout amenity.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: Umstead State Park free. NC state parks senior discount.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Wake County Public Library and Durham County Library free.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: GoRaleigh monthly pass ~$55 regular. Seniors 65+ ride for half-fare with Medicare card. GoTriangle regional routes connect Raleigh, Durham, and Chapel Hill. Chapel Hill Transit is fare-free.

publicTransit.coverage: GoRaleigh bus network covers Raleigh metro. GoDurham serves Durham. GoTriangle regional routes connect the three cities. Chapel Hill Transit is fare-free for all riders. Coverage is limited in suburban areas and between cities.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ get half-fare on GoRaleigh and GoDurham routes with Medicare card or senior ID. Chapel Hill Transit is free for everyone.

rideShare.availability: Good in Raleigh-Durham metro area. Uber and Lyft available. May have longer waits in suburban areas.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available for seniors. Trips between Raleigh and Durham can be $20-30.

registration.notes: North Carolina vehicle registration $36/year. Property tax on vehicles assessed by county. Wake County rate ~$0.60/$100 assessed value. On a $20,000 car this is ~$120/year. No state personal property tax beyond county levy.

insurance.notes: North Carolina car insurance slightly below national average. Raleigh-Durham rates moderate. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%).

fuel.notes: ~$3.30/gallon average in Raleigh-Durham. NC gas tax $0.405/gallon. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking in most suburban areas and Cary. Downtown Raleigh metered parking $1.50/hour. Monthly garage parking downtown $75-125.

tolls.notes: Minimal tolls in Raleigh-Durham area. NC 540 Triangle Expressway toll road ($1-5 per trip). Most daily driving avoids toll roads.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: North Carolina Safe Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). Wake County offers vehicle property tax relief for qualifying low-income seniors.

transportation.walkability: Moderate. Downtown Raleigh, downtown Durham, and Chapel Hill have walkable cores. Cary, North Hills, and most suburban areas are car-dependent. Extensive greenway trail network for recreation.

cycling.infrastructure: Growing bike lane network. Raleigh has 100+ miles of greenway trails. American Tobacco Trail (22 miles) in Durham. Cities actively expanding protected bike lanes.
cycling.notes: Raleigh greenway system is a major asset for recreation but not practical for most daily errands. Triangle area is mostly car-dependent.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: North Carolina Social Security Tax Exemption
discounts.description: North Carolina fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. No additional age deduction for other retirement income.

discounts.name: GoRaleigh / GoDurham Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Half-price bus fares for riders 65+. Present Medicare card or senior ID. Chapel Hill Transit is fare-free for all ages.

discounts.name: Raleigh Senior Centers
discounts.description: City of Raleigh operates 5 senior centers offering free programs, meals, health screenings, fitness classes, and social activities.

discounts.name: Wake County Tax Relief
discounts.description: Wake County offers property tax relief and deferral programs for seniors meeting income requirements. Homestead exemption available.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year). Raleigh is within driving distance of Blue Ridge Parkway, Great Smoky Mountains, and Cape Hatteras.

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. NC wireless tax ~8% + federal USF. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: North Carolina has no rental tax. Wake County property tax rate ~$0.60/$100 assessed value — landlord responsibility. No additional renter taxes or fees.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 2 months rent ($4,000). North Carolina Residential Rental Agreements Act applies.
housing.marketNotes: Research Triangle is a high-demand rental market driven by tech economy and university employment. Cary and North Raleigh popular for safety and amenities. Durham offers more character and lower prices. Prices have risen significantly but remain well below DC, NYC, or West Coast.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Raleigh-Durham has a strong Black community, especially around NCCU/Durham and SE Raleigh.

providers.name: A Nu U Salon
providers.notes: Natural hair, locs, color, treatments. Experienced with all textures. Black-owned.

providers.name: Bespoke Beauty Bar
providers.notes: Premium salon specializing in natural hair and color. By appointment.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: North Hills and Cameron Village areas have the best salon concentration.

providers.name: Douglas Carroll Salon
providers.notes: Top Raleigh salon. Award-winning colorists. Highlights, balayage.

providers.name: Arrow Salon
providers.notes: Modern salon with color specialists. Good for grey blending and mature hair.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL in Cary. Triangle Town Center and Crabtree Valley Mall for department store options.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Primary big & tall destination in the Triangle.

providers.name: Belk (Crabtree Valley Mall)
providers.notes: Extended sizes in men's department. Southern brands.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Crabtree Valley Mall and North Hills are the main shopping destinations. Tanger Outlets in Mebane for deals.

providers.name: Nordstrom (Crabtree Valley)
providers.notes: Full women's department. Regular sizes. Alterations.

providers.name: LOFT / J.Crew (North Hills)
providers.notes: Regular sizes. Work-appropriate and casual styles.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Good selection at Crabtree Valley Mall and Cary retail corridor.

providers.name: DSW (Cary/Raleigh)
providers.notes: Wide widths, extended sizes. Good clearance section.
